commands.review_summary:
The PR adds per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` overrides across config schema, docs, embedded/Codex compaction paths, memory flush, and focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows `AgentEntrySchema` does not accept per-agent `compaction` or `contextPruning`, and key compaction runtime paths still read `agents.defaults.compaction`; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is needed because the PR defines a new per-agent config contract and still lacks contributor real behavior proof.

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent compaction and contextPruning overrides so each configured agent can resolve compaction/runtime behavior from its active agent scope instead of always inheriting `agents.defaults`.

This is for multi-agent installations where main, channel/proxy, local/self-hosted, and Codex/app-server agents need different compaction model/provider/auth-profile, timeout, cache/pruning, thinking-level, and transcript-maintenance behavior.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related: #52751, #69988, #19501, #15826, #43834, #6571, #6572, #30452, #57901, #58077, #59863, #60512, #80784, #82008

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, per-agent compaction settings were either rejected by schema or only partially honored. Several runtime paths still read `agents.defaults.compaction` / `agents.defaults.contextPruning` even when the active `agents.list[]` entry had its own settings.

That broke or weakened these setups:

- main/default agent compacts with one model/provider while another agent compacts with another
- local/self-hosted agents use local provider aliases for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit an OpenClaw compaction model override and keep native runtime behavior
- legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys still need an explicit non-main `agentId` preserved across queued/direct compaction and maintenance paths
- queued/CLI/context-engine compaction should use `compaction.thinkingLevel`, not the active run's normal thinking level

## What Changed

- Adds strict schema/docs support for `agents.list[].compaction` and per-agent `contextPruning` semantics.
- Deep-merges per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` blocks over `agents.defaults`, so partial overrides inherit unspecified fields. `{}` means “override nothing,” not “clear inherited defaults.”
- Resolves compaction model/provider/auth-profile/timeout/cache/pruning/reserve/thinking settings through the active session agent.
- Threads `sessionAgentId` through queued/direct compaction, CLI compaction, context-engine maintenance, Codex app-server lifecycle, embedded attempt cache-TTL finalization, memory flush, transcript rotation, and follow-up/notice paths.
- Keeps explicit `compaction.model` behavior separate from Codex/app-server native runtime selection.
- Adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent scope in direct, queued, CLI, Codex lifecycle, cache-TTL, memory-flush, empty/partial merge, and compaction thinking-level propagation paths.

## Reviewer Notes

The key invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR explicitly passes `agentId` into compaction and maintenance params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed in current head: Per-agent compaction/contextPruning settings resolve from the active session agent in the repaired paths ClawSweeper flagged: empty-block inheritance, queued/CLI context-engine compaction thinking-level propagation, Codex lifecycle maintenance, embedded cache-TTL finalization, direct compaction fallback selection, and Codex-runtime preflight reserve-floor projection.
- Real environment tested for current head: Local OpenClaw source checkout on host `Nano-01`, branch based on `origin/main` at `1a7669bc63a0c3731577465b130873e849fc2773`, current PR head `8e9a247425c032ec466d7328ab4acd6b76db2339`.
- Exact commands run after latest patch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts \
  src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint \
  src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts \
  src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.queued.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts

node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
git diff --check
```

Evidence after latest fix:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs ...
 Test Files 1 passed (1)
 Tests 24 passed (24)
 Test Files 5 passed (5)
 Tests 162 passed (162)
 [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 112.38s

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint ...targeted files...
 Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
 [base-config-schema] ok

node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
 OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

git diff --check
 passed
```

Observed result after latest fix: Current head opts CLI and queued context-engine compaction builders into `compaction.thinkingLevel`, preserves empty-block inheritance for compaction thinking-level, and resolves Codex-runtime preflight compaction reserve floors via the same active-agent helper as Pi settings. Regressions prove the active non-main agent wins even for legacy-shaped session keys and empty per-agent compaction blocks inherit defaults.

What was not fully clean in this current-head pass: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --pretty false` no longer reports errors from this patch, but the broad test-src typecheck still fails on pre-existing `src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts` fixtures missing `configuredModel`, `selectedModel`, and `modelSelectionReason` fields from `SessionStatus`. I did not modify that unrelated status test surface in this PR.

## Supplemental Validation

Additional focused validation previously run on this branch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts
 Test Files 2 passed (2)
 Tests 58 passed (58)
 Test Files 1 passed (1)
 Tests 15 passed (15)
 [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 71.46s

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.agents.json --pretty false
 passed

npm run config:schema:check
 [base-config-schema] ok

npm run config:docs:check
 OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
```

## Current Branch State

- Base: `origin/main` / `1a7669bc63a0c3731577465b130873e849fc2773`
- Head: `8e9a247425c032ec466d7328ab4acd6b76db2339`
- GitHub reports the PR as mergeable; checks are expected to be queued/running after the push.
